findings.issue: OAuth handoff endpoints are public, but oauthApi.init/redeem are routed through makeRequest, which auto-injects any existing localStorage access_token as an Authorization header. A stale or unrelated bearer token is therefore sent to /v1/oauth/web/init and /v1/oauth/web/redeem, coupling pre-auth requests to prior auth state and unnecessarily exposing the token in request logs.
findings.remediation: Add a skip-auth option to makeRequest (e.g., { skipAuth: true }) or use raw fetch for the public OAuth init/redeem endpoints so no Authorization header is sent.

findings.issue: InvitationPage loads the invitation via raw fetch with no timeout and no AbortSignal. The 'active' flag only suppresses state updates on unmount; it does not cancel the network request, so a hanging server leaves the user on 'Loading invitation...' indefinitely and the request continues after the user navigates away.
findings.remediation: Route getInvitation through makeRequest (which has a 30s timeout) and thread an AbortSignal from the effect cleanup into the request so it is cancelled on unmount.

findings.issue: The Round 8 fix wraps the native crypto.subtle.digest call in try/catch so a throwing native digest falls back to sha256Fallback, but pkce.test.ts never covers that branch. A regression that removed the catch or that threw before the fallback would only be caught in manual/browser testing.
findings.remediation: Add a test that stubs crypto.subtle.digest to reject/throw and asserts generateChallenge still returns the expected SHA-256 hex via sha256Fallback.

findings.issue: AppRouter.test.tsx clears sessionStorage in beforeEach/afterEach but never clears localStorage. The callback success path writes access_token to localStorage, so leftover token state can leak into later tests if localStorage assertions are added or test ordering changes.
findings.remediation: Add localStorage.clear() to the beforeEach and afterEach hooks in AppRouter.test.tsx, matching the sessionStorage cleanup.

findings.issue: The verifier-generation compatibility test only checks that generateVerifier() returns a base64url-allowing string; it does not assert the required 43-character length or that base64 padding was removed. A regression that shortened the random buffer or that failed to strip '=' padding would still pass.
findings.remediation: Add assertions to the compatibility test: expect(verifier).toHaveLength(43) and expect(verifier).not.toContain('=').
